title: Cryptographic Randomness, Entropy, Keys, Salts, and Nonces · Runtime Contract: Crypto Go Entropy Vs Randomness
summary: A service seeds math/rand with time, generates keys and reset tokens from it, retries by reusing a nonce, and cannot prove fork or startup entropy behavior. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the entropy vs randomness increment in randomness and uniqueness contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and eviden…
objectives: Distinguish entropy sources, deterministic random bit generators, unpredictability, uniqueness, bias, and statistical appearance.
Use current Go cryptographic randomness APIs for keys, tokens, salts, nonces, and blinding while handling failure according to the API contract.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Predict Crypto Go Entropy Vs Randomness · Theory Crypto Go Entropy Vs Randomness
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory cryptographic engineering team handles case 2196 during a replicated device restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to budget cryptographic randomness and nonce uniqueness across use, restart, and rollback; cap bytes, work, memory, time, attempts, concurrency, and retained secret material before use; then rotate the active key while old data remains readable and new writes cannot downgrade. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
